hypothesis: Mutating position 1 to a positively charged residue (e.g., K) and position 11 to a hydrophobic residue (e.g., L) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs fail to achieve sufficient selectivity due to conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Targeted mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 are necessary to break off-target interactions while maintaining SSTR2 binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions with off-target SSTRs while preserving native-like interactions at key positions.
